untranslatable_reason: The Measure Rule signal requires calculation of 'formation height' (difference between highest high and trend-line price) and application of that calculated value to derive target prices. This requires: (1) identification of pattern formation boundaries (not available as indicators), (2) trend-line price calculation (not available as a standard indicator), and (3) dynamic arithmetic on formation geometry. The backtest engine's indicator set does not include trend-line price, formation height tracking, or the ability to store and reference calculated pattern measurements across bars. This signal is fundamentally a manual chart pattern analysis technique that cannot be fully operationalized with the available technical indicators.
translation_notes: BULKOWSKI_DRY_0 describes a conservative measure rule for chart patterns requiring manual identification of trend lines and formation geometry—not suitable for automated rule-based backtesting without custom pattern recognition indicators.
